# Canvas Checklist: Grid Canvas & Module Placement

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, completeness, and review-readiness of the canvas-layout requirements and supporting design artifacts before and during implementation review  
**Created**: 2026-04-22  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are drag requirements complete across drag start, snapped preview, valid release, invalid release, delayed persistence, and rollback outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015-028, UI Contract §Drag Contract]
  - Finding: Yes. Drag start, snapped preview, valid/invalid release, 500 ms delayed persistence, rollback, and server-error feedback are all explicitly covered.
- [x] CHK002 Are resize requirements complete for all eight handles, including how each handle may change origin, width, and height in grid units? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015-028, UI Contract §Resize Contract, Data Model §ResizeSession]
  - Finding: Partially. Eight handles and resize validation are defined, but handle-specific origin/axis math is left to implementation detail rather than explicitly specified.
- [x] CHK003 Are add, delete, and layering requirements complete for empty vs non-empty modules, no-space creation failures, and server-reconciled z-index results? [Completeness, Spec §FR-031-041, Spec §Edge Cases]
  - Finding: Yes. Empty vs non-empty delete, no-space creation failure, layering rules, and server z-index reconciliation are all covered.
- [x] CHK004 Are zoom and pan requirements fully specified, including plain-wheel panning behavior, viewport ownership, and any panning boundaries? [Gap, Spec §FR-042-046, UI Contract §Zoom and Pan Contract, Research §R7]
  - Resolution: Yes. User confirmed plain wheel should vertically pan inside the canvas viewport as a normative feature requirement.

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is “first available valid grid position” defined deterministically enough to avoid differing implementations when several valid slots exist? [Clarity, Spec §FR-032, Research §R5]
  - Finding: Yes. Placement order is defined as top-to-bottom, then left-to-right.
- [x] CHK006 Is the “short delayed save” requirement fully quantified and clearly scoped to the intended actions rather than left open to interpretation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-025-026, API Contract §PATCH /modules/{moduleId}/layout]
  - Finding: Yes. The delay is explicitly 500 ms and scoped to drag/resize layout persistence.
- [x] CHK007 Are notebook-metaphor styling terms such as “warm off-white,” “subtle page-edge lift,” “earthy,” and “muted terracotta” specific enough for objective review? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-006-007, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-023, UI Contract §Canvas Surface Contract]
  - Resolution: Yes. User requested concrete visual tokens or representative color/shadow targets to make review objective.
- [x] CHK008 Are feedback requirements specific about where and how validation, rollback, and success messages appear, or do they permit inconsistent UX surfaces? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-027-028, Spec §FR-034, Spec §FR-047, UI Contract §Feedback Contract]
  - Resolution: Yes. User selected toast-only as the canonical feedback surface for validation, rollback, and success outcomes.

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Are overlap rules consistent between occupied-cell wording, rectangle-intersection wording, and the exception for touching edges or corners? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-021-022, Spec §External Dependencies, Data Model §Validation rules]
  - Finding: Yes. All documents agree that intersecting rectangles are invalid while edge/corner contact remains valid.
- [x] CHK010 Are zoom interaction requirements consistent across the spec, research, and UI contract for buttons, keyboard shortcuts, Ctrl+wheel zoom, and plain-wheel pan? [Consistency, Spec §FR-042-046, Research §R7, UI Contract §Zoom and Pan Contract]
  - Finding: Yes. Buttons, keyboard shortcuts, Ctrl+wheel zoom, and plain-wheel pan are now aligned across the spec and supporting design artifacts.
- [x] CHK011 Are layering requirements consistent with the rule that z-index affects visual order only and never relaxes overlap validation? [Consistency, Spec §FR-039-041, UI Contract §Delete and Layering Contract]
  - Finding: Yes. Layering is consistently documented as visual-only and never a bypass for overlap rules.
- [x] CHK012 Are optimistic update and rollback requirements aligned across move, resize, create, delete, and server-returned z-index reconciliation flows? [Consistency, Spec §FR-024-028, Spec §FR-037, Spec §Edge Cases, API Contract §Query Integration Summary]
  - Finding: Yes. Optimistic update, rollback, and reconciliation behavior is consistently described across the major mutation flows.

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK013 Can drag and resize correctness be judged objectively from the current success criteria, or do they rely on subjective terms such as “user-intended” placement? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002, Spec §FR-018-023]
  - Finding: No. “User-intended grid position” remains subjective unless replaced with a more objective QA measure.
- [x] CHK014 Do acceptance criteria cover invalid add, delete, and layering flows with measurable outcomes rather than only positive-path completion? [Gap, Spec §SC-003-005, Spec §FR-033-047]
  - Finding: No. Invalid add is partially covered, but invalid delete/layering outcomes are not represented by measurable success criteria.
- [x] CHK015 Are notebook-styling fidelity expectations measurable enough for reviewers to determine whether the notebook metaphor has actually been met? [Measurability, Spec §SC-007, Spec §FR-006-007, Spec §FR-029, Spec §FR-038]
  - Finding: No. The metaphor is well described, but the current acceptance criteria do not define objective pass/fail styling thresholds.
- [x] CHK016 Are performance expectations specific enough for zoom, pan, and drag-frame responsiveness at the stated 10-20 module density? [Gap, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §Test Priorities, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-005]
  - Finding: No. Initial render and feedback latency are specified, but drag/pan smoothness lacks an objective responsiveness target.

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK017 Are requirements documented for drag and resize transitions at every supported zoom level, including snapped-coordinate stability and preserved selection state? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-046]
  - Finding: Yes. Zoom-scaled interactions, grid-stable snapping, and preserved selection state are documented.
- [x] CHK018 Are requirements documented for rapid repeated adjustments during the 500 ms delay window, including which final layout must persist if several edits occur close together? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-025-026, Research §R6]
  - Finding: No. Debounced persistence exists, but the final-wins rule for multiple quick successive edits is not explicitly stated.
- [x] CHK019 Are add-module requirements complete when different module types have different minimum sizes and title-related validation constraints? [Coverage, Spec §FR-030-034, Data Model §ModuleCreationRequest]
  - Finding: Yes. Module-type minimum sizes and title-related validation constraints are explicitly covered.
- [x] CHK020 Are delete and layering scenarios complete for selection state, restored module state after rollback, and post-reconciliation ordering? [Coverage, Spec §FR-035-041, Data Model §State Transitions]
  - Finding: Yes. Success/rollback selection behavior and post-save ordering expectations are sufficiently covered.

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Are exact-boundary-touch cases specified consistently for moves, resizes, and initial placement rather than only for drag release? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-019, Spec §FR-033]
  - Finding: Yes. Boundary-touch is consistently valid as long as the module remains fully in bounds.
- [x] CHK022 Are edge-touch and corner-touch cases defined as clearly for resize previews as they are for drag overlap logic? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-022-023, UI Contract §Resize Contract]
  - Finding: Yes. The same non-intersection rule governs resize previews and drag previews.
- [x] CHK023 Does the documentation define what happens if zoom changes during an active drag or resize session, or is that intentionally unsupported? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §User Story 4, UI Contract §Drag Contract, UI Contract §Resize Contract]
  - Resolution: Yes. User chose to disable zoom changes while a drag or resize session is active.
- [x] CHK024 Are rollback expectations defined for partial-failure cases where the UI updated optimistically but the authoritative server response normalizes layout or z-index differently? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, API Contract §POST /pages/{pageId}/modules, API Contract §PATCH /modules/{moduleId}/layout, API Contract §DELETE /modules/{moduleId}]
  - Finding: Yes. The docs consistently require reconciliation to the authoritative server response after optimistic updates.

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK025 Are accessibility requirements for keyboard access, focus visibility, resize-handle discoverability, and context-menu usage intentionally specified or explicitly out of scope? [Gap, Spec §FR-013-015, Spec §FR-042-043, UI Contract §Selection Contract]
  - Resolution: Yes. User put basic accessibility in scope: visible focus, keyboard access to zoom/add/context-menu actions, and proper labels.
- [x] CHK026 Are localization requirements for new labels, errors, and controls traceable from feature requirements rather than only implied by the implementation plan? [Gap, Plan §Constitution Check IX, Quickstart §Files to Modify]
  - Finding: Partially. Localization is enforced by project constitution and implementation plan, but not explicitly stated in the feature requirements.
- [x] CHK027 Are automated test-coverage expectations for utilities, hooks, components, and integration flows expressed as normative requirements or only as implementation guidance? [Traceability, Quickstart §Test Priorities, Plan §Constitution Check XII]
  - Finding: They are implementation guidance, not normative feature requirements.

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK028 Are backend contract assumptions explicit about authoritative reconciliation when returned server layout differs from optimistic client state? [Dependency, Spec §External Dependencies, API Contract §PATCH /modules/{moduleId}/layout]
  - Finding: Yes. Server-returned layout is authoritative and must reconcile optimistic client state.
- [x] CHK029 Is the single-editor assumption acceptable for review, or should stale-data and concurrent-edit requirements be added or explicitly deferred? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
  - Finding: Yes. Concurrent editing is explicitly deferred as out of scope for this feature.
- [x] CHK030 Are module-style and minimum-size dependencies pinned or described tightly enough to prevent silent contract drift across this feature’s lifetime? [Dependency, Spec §External Dependencies, Data Model §Module]
  - Finding: No. The dependencies are named clearly, but there is no explicit anti-drift mechanism beyond referencing the existing style/min-size sources.

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK031 Is “zoom/pan behavior” part of the normative feature requirements, or does pan remain only in supporting design artifacts and therefore ambiguous? [Conflict, Research §R7, UI Contract §Zoom and Pan Contract, Gap]
  - Resolution: Pan is part of the normative feature requirements. Plain wheel vertically pans inside the canvas viewport.
- [x] CHK032 Is there a clear traceability scheme linking drag, validation, rollback, styling, performance, and test artifacts back to requirement IDs, or is cross-document review still manual? [Traceability, Gap]
  - Finding: Cross-document traceability is still manual; there is no dedicated traceability matrix.

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link review findings back to specific FR/SC/contract sections when gaps are confirmed
- This checklist is for requirements/design quality review, not implementation verification
